provenance.source_corpus: Experimental reintroduction of the crayfish species Orconectes virilis into formerly acidified Lake 302S (Experimental Lakes Area, Canada)This paper is part of the series “Forty Years of Aquatic Research at the Experimental Lakes Area”.

nodes.name: reintroduction of the crayfish Orconectes virilis

source_spans.text: we experimentally reintroduced the crayfish Orconectes virilis into a biologically recovering, formerly acidified Boreal Shield lake

nodes.name: total biomass of other benthic invertebrates

source_spans.text: Orconectes virilis significantly (P < 0.05) suppressed the total biomass of other benthic invertebrates by 70% primarily because of declines in larval damselflies and midges.
primarily because of declines in larval damselflies and midges.
In addition, crayfish reduced periphytic biomass by 90% ( P < 0.001).

nodes.name: biomass of the most abundant odonate, Aeshna sp.

source_spans.text: Orconectes virilis enclosures contained significantly less biomass of the most abundant odonate, Aeshna sp.
However, crayfish significantly increased the population density of this anisopteran (RM-ANOVA, F = 4.37, P = 0.04)

nodes.name: total zygopteran biomass, consisting primarily of Enallagma sp.

source_spans.text: In contrast, crayfish significantly increased total zygopteran biomass, consisting primarily of Enallagma sp.
O. virilis reduced chlorophyll concentrations and altered taxonomic composition through disproportionately suppressing filamentous green algae containing the algal pigments chlorophyll b and lutein
suppressing filamentous green algae containing the algal pigments chlorophyll b and lutein
Similarly, crayfish significantly decreased benthic diatoms containing fucoxanthin and diatoxanthin.
